scope.selection: All installed production and development dependency artifacts selected by pnpm across the five workspace importers; workspace link packages are excluded.

evidence.licenseOutputSemantics: Each license-group entry can aggregate multiple versions; packageVersions expands every versions[] value to a unique name@version artifact.

policy.roleDerivation: Direct means declared in a workspace manifest; every other registry artifact is transitive. Production/development reachability comes from the corresponding pnpm recursive list graph.
policy.artifactReconciliation: Each expanded license packageVersion must have one exact pnpm-lock package record, at least one snapshot, SHA-512 integrity, and an installed graph tarball URL.
policy.licenseEvidence: License classification is reconciled between pnpm licenses output and installed package.json metadata; packaged license-file SHA-256 evidence is recorded when the artifact includes a root license file.
policy.redistribution: This Phase 0 file inventories locally installed dependencies only. No dependency package is vendored in the repository or redistributed in a release artifact.
policy.acceptedRisk: Moderate advisories require explicit package mapping, reachability rationale, review deadline, and an upstream-compatible remediation trigger; high/critical findings are not accepted.

advisories.title: ip-address has XSS in Address6 HTML-emitting methods

decision.rationale: The Phase 0 entrypoint imports only the stdio MCP path, not the HTTP rate-limit graph, and does not call the vulnerable Address6 HTML-emitting methods. express-rate-limit@8.4.1 pins ip-address exactly to 10.1.0, so forcing 10.1.1 would violate the upstream constraint.
decision.remediationTrigger: Upgrade when express-rate-limit or @modelcontextprotocol/sdk publishes an upstream-compatible graph containing ip-address>=10.1.1; re-run the audit sooner if the HTTP transport becomes reachable.
